title: Software Update - DFU - HS low battery: multiple clicks on buttons
scenario: DFU should be blocked when headset battery is below 5% and not on charger; rapid button clicks must not break app or dialog flow
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Connect a Bluetooth headset with battery level below 5% (and not plugged into power).
3. Navigate to Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings.
4. Locate the DFU (Device Firmware Update) option and tap it.
5. Observe that the DFU option is disabled or a warning is shown indicating low battery.
6. If the DFU flow is accessible despite low battery, rapidly tap the 'Update', 'Continue', or 'Start' buttons multiple times in quick succession.
7. Rapidly tap any dialog 'OK' buttons that appear during the DFU flow.
8. Observe the app and dialog behavior during and after the rapid clicks.
expected: DFU should not be possible when the headset battery is below 5% and not plugged into power. The app should either disable the DFU option or show a clear warning preventing the update. Rapid clicking on buttons should not crash the app, cause UI freezes, or break the dialog flow.
